summary: Census E1.6 completeness + E1.1 deferred CLI relocation-routing state

gaps.beadClassRead: WORK (convoy member work bead's own outbound source-dependency edges)
gaps.whyGap: drainProjectedBlockerIDs reads a drain MEMBER's outbound 'blocks' dependency edges via the plain ambient graph 'store'. A member is a work-class bead that E1.6 itself acknowledges may live in a different per-class store: the SAME function's reservation path (reserveDrainMember :1191-1209, releaseDrainReservations :1233-1247) deliberately routes member Get/SetMetadata through drainMemberOwningStore(store, member.ID, opts). A dependency edge is physically co-resident with its source bead (the residency model storeref encodes: 'a bead lives in exactly one store'), so after an E2 work-store relocation store.DepList(memberID) on the graph store returns EMPTY with no error -> the drain silently loses the member's source-dependency blockers. This read is NOT routed through drainMemberOwningStore / storeref.Resolve / opts.MemberStores; it is a genuine cross-store gap the E1.6 seam left uncovered.

gaps.beadClassRead: WORK (convoy member work bead's own outbound source-dependency edges)
gaps.whyGap: orderDrainMembersByDependencies reads each drain MEMBER's outbound 'blocks' edges via the plain ambient graph 'store' to compute the member dependency ordering. Same class/residency argument as :637: members are work-class beads potentially in the work store (the reservation path routes their Get/SetMetadata through drainMemberOwningStore), and a member's dependency edges live with the member. Unrouted store.DepList(memberID) on the graph store returns empty after E2 relocation, silently collapsing the topological ordering to insertion order. Not routed through drainMemberOwningStore / storeref.Resolve / opts.MemberStores -> genuine cross-store gap.

gaps.beadClassRead: cross-store SOURCE parent (sourceID = root.Metadata[gc.source_bead_id]) — a parent source bead that may live in another store per gc.source_store_ref
gaps.whyGap: resolveRequiredArtifactWorktree reads the retry workflow root's source parent bead on the ambient graph 'store'. When sourceID comes from gc.source_bead_id (not gc.input_convoy_id), it is the exact cross-store source-chain parent that walkSourceBeadChain (runtime.go :787-898) deliberately routes across stores via gc.source_store_ref + opts.ResolveStoreRef (e.g. a city-scope 'Adopt PR' request that spawned a rig-scope workflow). Here the source is fetched with a bare store.Get that ignores root.Metadata[gc.source_store_ref] and never consults opts.ResolveStoreRef, so after E2 the cross-store parent resolves to ErrNotFound and required-artifact validation degrades to 'missing_required_artifact_context'. Cross-store source read left unrouted by E1.6.

e16.notes: Enumerated all 43 raw store.Get/List/DepList across the 5 files (membership.go 7, convoy.go 3, drain.go 14, retry.go 6, runtime.go 13). Correctly-routed / already-seamed reads (not counted as (a) gaps): membership.go:33 & :131 (storeref.Resolve over memberProbeSet), drain.go:312 probe.Get inside drainMemberOwningStore, drain.go:1195 & :1237 reservation Get via owning store, drain.go:205/367 loadDrainManifestMembers (storeref.Resolve), drain.go:211 convoycore.Members(opts.MemberStores...), runtime.go:826 opts.ResolveStoreRef and :799/:847 walkSourceBeadChain reads over currentStore/nextStore resolved via resolvedStores. ~37 reads are genuine (a) SAME-STORE co-resident reads correctly on the ambient store: control bead itself, its molecule/workflow root (gc.root_bead_id), drain unit convoys, item roots, listByWorkflowRoot/listByWorkflowRootAndScope graph sweeps, scope body/subject, retry logical/eval/subject (graph step beads), resolveBlockedOutcome blockers (graph), and the convoy bead + its co-resident tracks/legacy-child edges (membership.go:56/97/122/169/191/202, convoy.go:88/124/142). The THREE (b) gaps all read a member/source bead of a DIFFERENT class from the ambient graph store. STRONGEST evidence is drain.go:637/:849: the identical member is routed through drainMemberOwningStore for reservation Get/SetMetadata in the same file, proving the seam authors knew members are cross-store yet left the member-DepList reads unrouted (a dependency edge is co-resident with its source bead). retry.go:436 is a real but graceful-degradation cross-store source read (walkSourceBeadChain routes the same gc.source_bead_id chain that this bare Get ignores). E1.6 is NOT complete.

The wrapper prepareWaitWakeStateForCity(store beads.Store) at :1006 boxes a raw store into beads.SessionStore{Store:store} — that is the CLI/test convenience seam. dep reads inside (depsWaitReadyDetailedForCity) are federated/plain and unaffected. No two-store split needed; controller path is correct today.

entangledHelpers.name: dispatchReadyWaitNudgesWithSnapshot(cityPath, cfg, store beads.Store, ...)
entangledHelpers.otherCallers: cmd/gc/city_runtime.go:2329 (controller) passes the RAW cr.cityBeadStore() (work store), NOT sessStore; cmd_wait_test.go
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: ENTANGLED. This helper mixes THREE classes on one store param: session reads (loadSessionBeadSnapshot, stampWaitLookupCapDiagnostic via sessionFrontDoor), wait writes (SetMetadata on wait bead), and nudge reads/writes (nudgeFrontDoor.Find, enqueueQueuedNudgeWithStore). Today all identity, so the raw work store works. The correct fix at E2 is to pass a session-class store for the session/wait reads and derive the nudge store internally via beads.NudgesStore{Store: resolveNudgesStore(...)}. This is a CONTROLLER-side change (city_runtime.go), NOT a cmd_wait.go CLI-root change — out of scope for E1.1 CLI relocation-routing.

entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Takes an already-built *session.Store front door, not a raw store. Session-class by construction. Callers own the routing; no split needed.

e11.routingPlan:
CLI-root scope for E1.1 is exactly the two openCityStore() roots (cmdSessionWait, cmdWaitSetStateResult); the two openStoreAtForCity fallbacks are the federated wait-list/inspect path and stay plain. Neither openCityStore root is whole-store — both are MULTI-CLASS and require SURGICAL routing.

ROOT A — cmdSessionWait (:193): cfg+cityPath are resolved at :218-222 AFTER the store open but BEFORE the first session-class consumer (:223), so no reordering is needed to compute sessStore in time. Surgical plan: compute `sessStore := cliSessionStore(store, cfg, cityPath)` at ~:222 (right after cfg load), then route the SESSION/WAIT consumers to sessStore: resolveSessionIDWithConfig(:223 store->sessStore), sessionFrontDoor(store)(:228) -> cliSessionFrontDoor(store,cfg,cityPath) or sessionFrontDoor(sessStore), store.Create wait bead(:256 ->sessStore), setWaitTerminalState(:272 ->sessStore), store.SetMetadataBatch ready(:283 ->sessStore), sessionFrontDoor(store).ApplyPatch(:294 -> cliSessionFrontDoor). LEAVE plain: loadWaitDependencyBead(:234) and depsWaitReadyDetailedForCity(:270) — these are work-class dep reads that walk convoy scope candidates via openStoreAtForCity (federated), and must read the WORK store where the dep beads live, NOT the session-class store.

ROOT B — cmdWaitSetStateResult (:642): NO cfg/cityPath in scope at open time (resolveCity is called lazily at :708 inside the canceled branch). Per the CONTEXT rule for cfg-less roots, load cfg via loadCityConfigWithoutBuiltinPackRefresh(cityPath, io.Discard) after a resolveCity() — nil cfg still yields identity/byte-identical today. Surgical plan: compute sessStore once, route the WAIT/SESSION consumers: store.Get(waitID)(:646 ->sessStore, it is a wait bead), retryClosedWait(:663 ->sessStore for its session/wait ops; its internal nudgeFrontDoor derives nudge store from the passed store), store.SetMetadataBatch/apply(:697,:700 ->sessStore), clearSessionWaitHoldIfIdle(:714 ->sessStore). LEAVE on the passed store for the NUDGE leaf: nextWaitDeliveryAttempt(:679) wraps beads.NudgesStore{Store: store} — at E2 that inner wrap should route via resolveNudgesStore, but since both classes are identity today and the nudge wrap is derived INSIDE the helper from whatever store is passed, passing sessStore there is still byte-identical today; cleanest is to keep the nudge derivation reading the plain work store. Practically: because sessStore and the plain store are the SAME value today, either assignment is byte-identical; document that the nudge lookups are nudge-class and belong on resolveNudgesStore at E2.

The 40+ internal helpers (loadWaitBeads, prepareWaitWakeState*, dispatch*, finalize*, retryClosedWait, setWaitTerminalState, clearSessionWaitHold*, etc.) do NOT open stores themselves — they inherit the store from their caller. They need NO edits in E1.1; their routing is determined entirely at the two CLI roots (and, separately, at the controller call sites in city_runtime.go, which are E2/controller scope, not this CLI task)."
e11.risks:
BYTE-IDENTITY: resolveSessionStore/resolveNudgesStore/resolveClassStore are pure identity stubs today (class_store.go:231 returns workStore verbatim; nil cfg => identity), so every sessStore/nudgeStore reassignment is byte-identical until E2 flips the boundary. Safe now.

CROSS-CLASS / LEAVE-ON-PLAIN: The dep reads (loadWaitDependencyBead:934, depsWaitReadyDetailedForCity:886) are WORK-class and FEDERATED — they iterate convoyStoreCandidates and open per-scope stores via openStoreAtForCity. Routing these to the session-class store would break dep resolution once sessions relocate (session store would not hold user work/convoy dep beads). Must stay plain. This is the primary trap in this file.

FEDERATED WAIT-LIST/INSPECT: doWaitListFallback:392 and doWaitInspectFallback:507 deliberately open via openStoreAtForCity(cityPath,cityPath). These are marked 'undecided' because wait beads ARE session-class (gc:wait->ClassSessions) and after relocation they would live in the session store — BUT the fallback path is a separate design decision (federated read of the whole city) and is NOT an openCityStore root. Recommend deferring these to a follow-up: they need routing through cliSessionStore too once relocation lands, but doing so now risks diverging from the API/primary read path which reads by label across the city. Flag, do not change in E1.1.

TEST-CALLER RIPPLE: cmd_wait_test.go calls nearly every helper directly with a raw store. Since helpers are not being re-signatured (only the two CLI roots gain a local sessStore), tests are unaffected. readyWaitSetForList/prepareWaitWakeStateForCityWithSnapshot/dispatchReadyWaitNudgesWithSnapshot keep their signatures; no test churn.

CONTROLLER ENTANGLEMENT (out of scope but note): dispatchReadyWaitNudgesWithSnapshot at city_runtime.go:2329 is passed the RAW work store (not cr.sessionsBeadStore()) while prepareWaitWakeStateForCityWithSnapshot at :2255 is passed sessStore. This asymmetry means the wait-nudge dispatch reads session+wait beads off the work store. It is a real pre-existing controller routing gap, but it is E2/controller-side, not a CLI relocation root — the DEFERRED reason in the task (shared with the reconciler) is exactly this.
e11.recommendation: Tractable but genuinely surgical, not whole-store. Do BOTH openCityStore roots surgically: (1) cmdSessionWait — add sessStore after the existing cfg load (:222) and route the 6 session/wait consumers to it, KEEP the 2 dep-read consumers (loadWaitDependencyBead:234, depsWaitReadyDetailedForCity:270) on the plain work store. (2) cmdWaitSetStateResult — resolveCity + loadCityConfigWithoutBuiltinPackRefresh to get cfg/cityPath, add sessStore, route store.Get/retryClosedWait/apply/clearSessionWaitHoldIfIdle to sessStore; the nudge lookup inside nextWaitDeliveryAttempt is nudge-class and its NudgesStore wrap is derived from the passed store, so it stays byte-identical today (document as resolveNudgesStore at E2). Do NOT touch the internal helpers (they inherit routing), the two federated fallbacks (doWaitListFallback/doWaitInspectFallback — flag as follow-up), or the controller call sites in city_runtime.go (E2). withdrawQueuedWaitNudges is already class-correct (self-opens nudge store). Guard: the relocation guard test (TestSessionRelocationRootsRouteThroughSessionClassStore) will want the sessionFrontDoor(store) forms at these roots replaced by cliSessionFrontDoor(...); use that helper for the two sessionFrontDoor call sites (:228, :294) inside cmdSessionWait.

entangledHelpers.name: doHandoffWithOutcome (and its wrapper doHandoff)
entangledHelpers.otherCallers: cmd_handoff_test.go lines 77,431,504,544,574 (5 call sites, all pass a single beads.NewMemStore() used for BOTH mail-create and session-restartable reads). Prod caller: cmdHandoff:162.
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: MAIL+SESSION entangled on ONE store param. Preferred: keep the single store param as the MAIL/plain store and add a second sessStore beads.Store param (two-store split), OR route internally: inside doHandoffWithOutcome derive nothing (no cfg/cityPath in scope) — so the routed sessStore must be PASSED IN from cmdHandoff (which has cfg+cityPath). Threading a sessStore param ripples to all 5 test call sites; at the single-store bd backend tests keep passing the same memstore for both args, so they stay byte-identical. Do NOT try to compute cliSessionStore inside the helper — it lacks cfg/cityPath.

entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Same MAIL+SESSION entanglement: createHandoffMail(store) is MAIL, the remaining 6 session ops are SESSION. Add a sessStore param passed from cmdHandoffRemote (has cfg@194 + cityPath@189); tests pass the same memstore twice (byte-identical today).

entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: PURE SESSION (contains the forbidden sessionFrontDoor(store) @377). Its store param must receive sessStore; then line 377 becomes sessionFrontDoor(sessStore) — an already-routed local, which the guard allows (only the literal sessionFrontDoor(store) is forbidden).

e11.routingPlan:
SURGICAL in all three roots (each root is MULTI-CLASS: it opens ONE work store consumed by BOTH mail-class and session-class sites). Compute a routed session store once per root and thread it ONLY to session consumers; keep the plain opened `store` for the mail path.

cmd_handoff.go — cmdHandoff (root @146): cfg already loaded @159 as `cfg` and cityPath = current.cityPath. Compute `sessStore := cliSessionStore(store, cfg, current.cityPath)` (move/duplicate the cfg load ahead of line 160 if needed; it currently sits at 159 which is already before use). Route: sessionRestartPersister(...,sessStore,...) @160; and thread sessStore into doHandoffWithOutcome as a new param. Inside doHandoffWithOutcome: keep createHandoffMail on plain `store` (@234), pass sessStore to sessionRestartableByController(@239) and clearRestartRequest(@245). doHandoffAuto (@154) stays on plain store (pure mail).

cmd_handoff.go — cmdHandoffRemote (root @185): cfg loaded @194, cityPath @189. Compute `sessStore := cliSessionStore(store, cfg, cityPath)` after line 194. resolveDefaultMailSenderForCommand @195 does a session read (sender identity) — route it to sessStore. Thread sessStore into doHandoffRemote (new param). Inside doHandoffRemote: keep createHandoffMail on plain store (@391); route the 6 session ops (@396,402,411,423,424,428) to sessStore.

cmd_runtime_drain.go — cmdRuntimeRequestRestart (root @506): cfg loaded @526 (move ahead of first session use @511) or add a cfg load; cityPath = current.cityPath. Compute `sessStore := cliSessionStore(store, cfg, current.cityPath)` BEFORE line 511. Route: sessionRestartableByController(sessStore,...) @511; clearRestartRequest(sessStore, dops,...) @517; workerHandleForSessionTargetWithConfig(current.cityPath, sessStore, sp, cfg,...) inside the persistRestart closure @530. This file has NO mail-class use, so it is effectively whole-session for the store — but keep it surgical/explicit since the store is still opened generically.

Shared session helpers (sessionRestartableByController, clearRestartRequest, sessionRestartPersister) are private to these two files with no external callers — they need no signature change beyond receiving the already-routed sessStore from each caller. doHandoffWithOutcome + doHandoffRemote DO need a new sessStore param because their test callers pass a store directly.

Guard follow-up: add both files to sessionRelocationRoutedFiles in frontdoor_di_guard_test.go once routed; the guard then requires a cliSessionStore(/cliSessionFrontDoor( call present and forbids the literal sessionFrontDoor(store) (currently present at cmd_handoff.go:377 inside clearRestartRequest) — that becomes sessionFrontDoor(sessStore).
e11.risks:
BYTE-IDENTITY: resolveSessionStore is identity today (resolveClassStore -> work store at single-store bd), and both roots already call loadCityConfig, so cliSessionStore(store,cfg,cityPath) returns `store` verbatim — every routed call is byte-identical now. cfg is cfg-BEARING command roots (user-attended, not hot/hook/daemon), and they ALREADY use loadCityConfig today; do NOT switch to loadCityConfigWithoutBuiltinPackRefresh (would change today's pack-refresh behavior — a byte-identity regression in the opposite direction). Passing nil cfg would also be identity but is unnecessary since cfg is in scope.

CROSS-CLASS / LEAVE-ON-PLAIN: createHandoffMail -> beadmail.New(store) is MAIL — must stay on the plain opened store, NOT sessStore. doHandoffAuto is pure mail — leave on plain. (No nudge/dep/rigStores maps in either file.)

MIXED-HELPER SPLIT: doHandoffWithOutcome and doHandoffRemote each carry ONE store used for BOTH mail (create) and session (restart) — the split must give createHandoffMail the mail store and everything else the sessStore. Getting this backwards silently sends mail writes to the session backend after relocation.

TEST RIPPLE: adding a sessStore param to doHandoffWithOutcome (5 test sites) and doHandoffRemote (5 test sites) touches cmd_handoff_test.go, session_lifecycle_payload_test.go, telemetry_lifecycle_metrics_test.go. Tests use beads.NewMemStore() and must pass the same memstore for BOTH the mail store and sessStore args to preserve current single-store semantics. createHandoffMail's own test (cmd_handoff_seam_test.go:25) is unaffected (pure mail param unchanged).

ORDERING: in cmdRuntimeRequestRestart the cfg load is at 526 but the first session use is 511 — the cfg load (and sessStore construction) must be hoisted above line 511, or a session-store read would occur before cfg is available. Same care in cmdHandoff (cfg@159 is already before the @160/@162 uses — fine).

GUARD: cmd_handoff.go:377 currently holds the literal sessionFrontDoor(store) forbidden needle; if the file is added to sessionRelocationRoutedFiles before that line is converted to sessionFrontDoor(sessStore), the guard fails.
e11.recommendation: Route both files together as one E1.1 unit (they share three private session helpers and two entangled mail+session helpers). SURGICAL routing in all three roots. Concrete steps: (1) In each root compute sessStore := cliSessionStore(store, cfg, cityPath) once, after cfg is loaded and before the first session consumer (hoist the cfg load in cmdRuntimeRequestRestart above line 511). (2) Add a sessStore beads.Store param to doHandoffWithOutcome/doHandoff and doHandoffRemote; inside them keep createHandoffMail on the existing mail store and pass sessStore to every session op (sessionRestartableByController, clearRestartRequest, workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig, sessionAgentMetricIdentityByName, workerKillSessionTargetWithConfig, resolveSessionID). (3) Pass sessStore to sessionRestartPersister @160 and resolveDefaultMailSenderForCommand @195. (4) Convert clearRestartRequest line 377 sessionFrontDoor(store) -> sessionFrontDoor(sessStore) via the routed param. (5) Update the 10 test call sites to pass the same memstore for both mail+sessStore args. (6) Add cmd_handoff.go and cmd_runtime_drain.go to sessionRelocationRoutedFiles in frontdoor_di_guard_test.go. All changes are byte-identical at today's single-store backend.

entangledHelpers.name: tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller
entangledHelpers.otherCallers: cmd/gc/nudge_dispatcher.go:189. MIXED helper: nudge front-door writes + session front-door (SetMarker) + wait-bead reads + session handle.
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Two-store split candidate. Cleanest: give it BOTH a nudgeStore and a session-routed store (or pass the already-split stores from callers). Because it internally re-opens via openNudgeBeadStore fallback (line 1260) for nudge ops and derives sessFront from the same .Store, splitting means: nudge ops keep the nudge store; sessionFrontDoor + workerHandleForNudgeTarget + blockedQueuedNudgeReason(wait) route to sessStore. Coordinate with nudge_dispatcher.go census.

entangledHelpers.name: deliverSessionNudge / sendMailNotify

entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: These open openNudgeBeadStore themselves and pass .Store into the *WithWorker helpers whose leaf ops are SESSION-class. Convert the store passed to the worker helper to sessStore (whole-store per call). Entry points unchanged; external callers unaffected.

e11.routingPlan:
SURGICAL, per-root. This file is fundamentally NUDGE-class (openNudgeBeadStore returns beads.NudgesStore, already nudges-relocation-routed via resolveNudgesStore), but it performs SESSION-class reads/writes on that store's underlying .Store for nudge ADDRESSING/DELIVERY. E2 divergence: if [beads.classes.sessions] relocates but nudges do not, the two .Store values differ, so session reads on the nudge store hit the wrong backend. Plan: keep the nudge front-door path on the NudgesStore, and route ONLY the session leaves to a session-routed store built from the SAME opened work store.

Because openNudgeBeadStore hides the raw work store and re-wraps it as NudgesStore, introduce a companion CLI opener or unwrap: for each root that also does session ops, compute sessStore := cliSessionStore(deliveryStore.Store, target.cfg, target.cityPath) and sessFront := cliSessionFrontDoor(deliveryStore.Store, target.cfg, target.cityPath). Then:
- cmdNudgeDrainWithFormat: sessFront from cliSessionFrontDoor (replace line 455); split arm at 462 (blockedQueuedNudgeReason -> wait/session) takes sessStore; keep the two recordQueuedNudgeFailureWithStore (459/505) on deliveryStore (nudge).
- cmdNudgePoll: nudgeObserveTarget at 643 takes sessStore; tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller (674) needs the two-store split (nudge + session).
- deliverSessionNudgeWithWorker family: the store threaded to shouldQueueManagedNudgeWake/nudgeObserveTarget/workerHandleForNudgeTarget/requestManagedNudgeWake/loadSessionBeads/blockedQueuedNudgeReason -> sessStore; enqueue* / nudgeFrontDoor -> nudgeStore.
- resolveNudgeTarget: the session reads at 1089/1091 -> sessStore (cfg loaded at 1083, cityPath in scope).
- The 8 pure nudge-queue roots (claimDueQueuedNudgesMatching, listQueuedNudges, listQueuedNudgesForTarget, enqueueQueuedNudgeWithStore, ackQueuedNudgesWithOutcome, releaseQueuedNudgeClaims, recordQueuedNudgeFailureDetailed) stay entirely on the NudgesStore — NO change.

Because the mixed session/nudge helpers (enqueueManagedNudgeThenWake, queueManagedSessionNudgeWake, tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller, deliverSessionNudgeWithWorker) receive a single store today and use it for BOTH classes, the surgical fix requires threading two routed stores (or the pre-split sessStore alongside the NudgesStore) through them.
e11.risks: Byte-identity: all resolve*Store are identity today (resolveClassStore is a stub), so cliSessionStore/cliSessionFrontDoor(store,cfg,cityPath) return the same underlying store; NudgesStore already wraps that same store. So the split is byte-identical NOW and only diverges post-E2. Risk: nudge_dispatcher.go:189 and cmd_sling.go call the MIXED/session helpers (tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller, workerObserveNudgeTarget, workerHandleForNudgeTarget, withNudgeTargetFence, stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt, enqueueQueuedNudgeWithStore) with their OWN stores — if this file changes those helper SIGNATURES to split stores, every external caller + tests (clock_inject_test.go, nudge_dispatcher_test.go, nudge_target_info_equiv_test.go) ripples. Prefer caller-side routing (keep signatures) so the ripple is confined to each file's own census. cfg-less roots (the 8 nudge-queue helpers) are correctly LEFT on nudge store (no session ops); do NOT thread cfg there. loadCityConfig side-effect: resolveNudgeTarget already calls loadCityConfig (line 1083, not the pack-refresh-free variant) — cfg is available on target.cfg, so no new load needed at the drain/poll roots. No map[string]beads.Store rigStores in this file. The gc:wait reads (blockedQueuedNudgeReason) are SESSION-class per coordclass (labelWait->ClassSessions) — do NOT leave them on the nudge store; a nudge/session relocation split would strand wait lookups.
e11.recommendation: Route this file SURGICALLY. Two categories: (1) the ~8 pure nudge-queue roots that open openNudgeBeadStore and only touch nudgeFrontDoor/markQueuedNudgeTerminal stay entirely on the beads.NudgesStore — leave untouched. (2) The session-addressing/delivery leaves (sessionFrontDoor builds at 455/1050/1264; store.Get/resolveSessionIDMaterializingNamed at 1089/1091; nudgeObserveTarget/workerObserveNudgeTarget; workerHandleForNudgeTarget; requestManagedNudgeWake+session.WakeSession; loadSessionBeads; blockedQueuedNudgeReason wait reads; stampLastNudgeDeliveredAt SetMarker) route to a session store built via cliSessionStore / cliSessionFrontDoor(deliveryStore.Store, target.cfg, target.cityPath). Keep every entangled helper's SIGNATURE stable and route at the call site (pass sessStore for the session leaf, keep the NudgesStore for nudge ops), so external callers in cmd_sling.go / nudge_dispatcher.go / cmd_wait.go / cmd_mail.go and the three test files are handled in their own censuses without a cross-file signature cascade. The MIXED helpers (enqueueManagedNudgeThenWake, queueManagedSessionNudgeWake, tryDeliverQueuedNudgesByPoller, deliverSessionNudgeWithWorker, sendMailNotifyWithWorker) each need BOTH a session-routed store and the NudgesStore threaded through — do these as a coordinated two-store pass. Because everything is byte-identical today, land as a mechanical E1.1 refactor with the relocation guard test (TestSessionRelocationRootsRouteThroughSessionClassStore) extended to cover these roots.

rootsOpeningStore.opensVia: openStoreAtForCity(storeDir, cityPath) — SOURCE-bead branch (sourceBead.exists). storeDir=sourceBead.storeDir (rig/city scope by bead prefix). Generic WORK store.

slingOpenCityStore(cityPath) (= openCityStoreAt, var at line 171). Opens the CITY generic WORK store as a fallback pool-nudge store when the deps.Store had no running instance. cityPath in scope; cfg in scope (param). Feeds tryNudgeStore -> resolvePoolSessionRefs/workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig (SESSION reads) and later nudge writes (NUDGES).

printNudgePreview(w, a, deps.CityName, deps.SP, deps.Store, deps.Cfg) — SESSION liveness preview

This is a two-store-in-deps split (add a session-class store to SlingDeps/GraphRouteDeps) and is out of scope for a single-file surgical E1.1 pass. Classify session, defer the plumbing.

e11.routingPlan:
SURGICAL, not whole-store. The two store roots in cmdSlingWithJSON (source store @314/319 = deps.Store) are genuinely MULTI-CLASS: the same store value is used for WORK writes (Create@349, SetMetadata routed_to@653), WORK/GRAPH dry-run reads (Get, convoy Members, FindBlockingMolecule), AND session reads via the nudge path. So the opened store must STAY on plain 'store' for its WORK/GRAPH/work-query consumers, and ONLY the session consumers get routed.

Concrete surgical sites — route through cliSessionStore(store,cfg,cityPath) / cliSessionFrontDoor(...):
1. doSlingNudge (the session hub): compute sessStore := cliSessionStore(store,cfg,cityPath) at entry (cfg+cityPath already params) and use it for resolvePoolSessionRefs@1378, workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig@1380, buildSlingNudgeTarget@1389/@1416, the fixed-agent lookupSessionNameOrLegacy@1415, and the deliverSlingNudge store arg@1390/@1417. The slingOpenCityStore(cityPath)@1400 fallback likewise routes cliSessionStore over the opened city store before tryNudgeStore. Keep the NUDGES enqueue (deliverSlingNudge:1504 beads.NudgesStore{Store: store}) on a NON-session store (its own NUDGES routing, separate effort).
2. deliverSlingNudge: the sessionFrontDoor(store)@1495 (the DEFERRED site) becomes cliSessionFrontDoor(store,cfg,cityPath) — BUT deliverSlingNudge has no cfg/cityPath params today (only cityPath). Either thread cfg into deliverSlingNudge, or have doSlingNudge pass an ALREADY-session-routed store into deliverSlingNudge so the inner sessionFrontDoor(store) is over the session store (simpler, byte-identical). The workerObserve/workerHandle session reads @1479/@1483 ride the same routed store.
3. printNudgePreview@1820/@1821 (dry-run): route deps.Store through cliSessionStore(deps.Store, deps.Cfg, deps.CityPath) for the two session reads. Callers at dryRunSingle:1653 / dryRunBatch:1739 have deps in scope.

LEAVE on plain 'store': Create@349, SetMetadata@653 (WORK), all Get/List/Members/ProbeBeadInStore (WORK), FindBlockingMolecule@1806 (GRAPH children of a WORK bead — its own GRAPH routing effort, not session). 

DEFER: cliDirectSessionResolver@595 session read — the store arrives via internal/sling+internal/graphroute deps threading; needs a two-store SlingDeps split, out of scope for this file's surgical pass. resolveGraphStepBinding*@1298/@1318 session reads belong to cmd_convoy_dispatch.go's root, not any sling root — route there.
e11.risks:
BYTE-IDENTITY: cliSessionStore/cliSessionFrontDoor are identity today (nil-or-single-store => resolveSessionStore returns the store verbatim), so every surgical swap is byte-identical until a [beads.classes.sessions] relocation is configured. Low risk.

CROSS-CLASS MAP / LEAVE-PLAIN: (a) the deps.Store is deliberately multi-class — DO NOT whole-store convert it; routing SetMetadata routed_to@653 or Create@349 to the session store would MISROUTE work-bead writes after relocation. (b) The NUDGES enqueue at deliverSlingNudge:1504 must stay on a non-session store (NUDGES class) — feeding it sessStore would relocate nudge shadow beads onto the session backend. (c) FindBlockingMolecule@1806 is GRAPH-class (molecule/wisp children) — leave plain; it is a separate GRAPH-routing effort.

CFG-LESS PATH: deliverSlingNudge lacks cfg param; to route its sessionFrontDoor@1495 either thread cfg or (preferred) pass a pre-routed session store from doSlingNudge. If cfg must be loaded locally in any cfg-less nudge path, use loadCityConfigWithoutBuiltinPackRefresh(cityPath, io.Discard) — but here cfg is already available upstream in cmdSlingWithJSON, so thread it rather than reload.

TEST RIPPLE: doSlingNudge (cmd_sling_test.go:1223/1291) and deliverSlingNudge (cmd_nudge_test.go:3138/3189) pass a raw store; if deliverSlingNudge gains a cfg param its signature changes and those tests need updating. resolvePoolSessionRefs already takes a store (main_test.go x8) so no signature change there. cmd_stop.go:307 already routes sessStore into resolvePoolSessionRefs — established precedent, no new test churn.

ENTANGLEMENT: cliDirectSessionResolver and resolveGraphStepBinding* look like this file's session consumers but their store originates from OTHER roots (graphroute deps / cmd_convoy_dispatch.go) — routing them here would be wrong; must be handled in their owning census/effort.
e11.recommendation: Do the SURGICAL routing of the nudge hub in this file: introduce sessStore := cliSessionStore(store,cfg,cityPath) inside doSlingNudge and route the six session consumers (resolvePoolSessionRefs, workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig, buildSlingNudgeTarget x2, lookupSessionNameOrLegacy, deliverSlingNudge store arg) plus the slingOpenCityStore fallback through it; convert the DEFERRED sessionFrontDoor(store)@1495 to cliSessionFrontDoor by passing an already-session-routed store into deliverSlingNudge (avoids adding a cfg param); route printNudgePreview's two session reads via cliSessionStore(deps.Store,deps.Cfg,deps.CityPath). Keep WORK (Create, SetMetadata routed_to, Get/List/Members/Probe), the NUDGES enqueue, and the GRAPH FindBlockingMolecule on plain 'store'. Do NOT touch cliDirectSessionResolver or resolveGraphStepBinding* here — they are entangled across internal/sling+internal/graphroute and cmd_convoy_dispatch.go respectively and belong to separate efforts.

Leaf class = SESSION.

Internally unwraps store:=sessStore.Store for SESSION writes (RepairType/close/setMeta via session.NewStore) AND takes rigStores for cross-class work-release (closeSessionBeadIfUnassigned / closeSessionBeadIfRuntimeStoppedAndUnassigned).
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Already accepts beads.SessionStore at the boundary. Route by constructing the wrapper over the session-class store: beads.SessionStore{Store: cliSessionStore(oneShotStore,cfg,cityPath)}. rigStores stays the plain per-rig map (cross-class work-release). Daemon caller already feeds a routed store; no helper change.

entangledHelpers.otherCallers: city_runtime.go (daemon), session_reconciler.go (self/wrappers), session_reconciler_test.go. Internally wraps beads.SessionStore{Store: store} for the SESSION arm; also consumes rigStores + assignedWorkBeads (WORK) + does work-release.
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Pass cliSessionStore(oneShotStore,cfg,cityPath) as the `store` arg (it is re-wrapped to SessionStore inside). rigStores + assignedWorkBeads stay plain. Daemon path already routes its store; test callers unaffected (nil cfg → identity).

entangledHelpers.name: releaseOrphanedPoolAssignmentsWhenSnapshotsComplete(store beads.Store, ..., rigStores)
entangledHelpers.otherCallers: city_runtime.go (daemon), pool_session_name.go (self), cmd_start_test.go. `store` is used for a SESSION read (liveOpenSessionAssignmentExists(store,assignee) at pool_session_name.go:194) but the WRITES are WORK-class (releaseOrphanedPoolAssignment on ownerStore/assignedWorkStores/rigStores).
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Predominantly WORK — LEAVE on plain oneShotStore. Its lone session read is a liveness cross-check; splitting it out would need a second store param. Defer: acceptable to keep plain since work-release must target the work store, and the session read is identity today.

entangledHelpers.name: buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads(store beads.Store, rigStores, ...)
entangledHelpers.otherCallers: city_runtime.go (daemon), cmd_supervisor.go, build_desired_state.go (self), scale_from_zero*_test.go, city_runtime_test.go, build_desired_state_test.go. Multi-class: SESSION read via collectAllOpenSessionBeads(cfg,store,rigStores,...) (session.ListAllSessionBeads fanned across city store + rig stores) AND WORK-bead demand (AssignedWorkBeads) computed off the same store.
entangledHelpers.splitStrategy: Genuinely mixed — the session-snapshot arm and the work-demand arm read the SAME `store` param. Cannot be cleanly routed by swapping the single `store` arg without splitting the function into a session-store arg + work-store arg (a two-store split inside build_desired_state.go). This is the mirror-of-runtime core; defer to a dedicated E2 pass shared with the daemon.

e11.routingPlan:
SURGICAL, not whole-store. The opened oneShotStore is genuinely MULTI-CLASS: it feeds session snapshot/sync (SESSION), work-bead demand + orphan release (WORK), and rig-store fan-out (cross-class). Do NOT compute a single sessStore and blanket-replace `store`.

Cleanly routable session-arm sites (route now, low risk):
  - :896 loadSessionBeadSnapshot(oneShotStore) → loadSessionBeadSnapshot(sessStore)
  - :954 loadSessionBeadSnapshot(oneShotStore) → sessStore
  - :907/:921/:962 syncSessionBeadsWithSnapshotAndRigStores: wrap beads.SessionStore{Store: sessStore} (keep rigStores plain)
  - :942 reconcileSessionBeadsAtPathWithNamedDemand: pass sessStore as the `store` arg (keep rigStores + assignedWorkBeads plain)
  where sessStore := cliSessionStore(oneShotStore, cfg, cityPath) computed once right after oneShotStore is finalized (after the :887 else-branch, so it wraps both the real store and the MemStore). cfg + cityPath are already in scope.

LEAVE on plain oneShotStore (cross-class / work-class):
  - :892 buildStandaloneRigStores → rigStores map (cross-class, per-rig WORK stores)
  - :911 releaseOrphanedPoolAssignmentsWhenSnapshotsComplete (WORK-write dominant; lone session read is identity today)
  - :929/:941 filterAssignedWorkBeadsFor* (pure filters, no store)
  - :905/:920/:961 configuredSessionNamesWithSnapshot (snapshot-only, no store)

DEFER (undecided — the real mirror-of-runtime blocker):
  - :902/:918/:959 buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads reads BOTH session snapshot (collectAllOpenSessionBeads over store+rigStores) and work demand off the SAME `store` arg. Routing requires a two-store split of build_desired_state.go shared with the daemon (city_runtime.go), so it is out of scope for a cmd_start-local E1.1 surgical pass.

The adoption barrier (:880) is ALREADY routed via cliSessionFrontDoor (CONT-40).
e11.risks:
1) BYTE-IDENTITY: cliSessionStore/cliSessionFrontDoor = resolveSessionStore(store,cfg,cityPath,nil) which is an IDENTITY stub at the default single-store backend, so every routed site is byte-identical today; only diverges once [beads.classes.sessions] relocates in E2. cfg is non-nil here (loaded via loadStartCityConfig at :667), so routing is live-capable, still identity today.
2) MEMSTORE PATH: sessStore must be computed AFTER the :887 else-branch sets oneShotStore = NewMemStore(), or the MemStore fallback would bypass routing. Compute once at ~:892.
3) CROSS-CLASS MAP: rigStores (map[string]beads.Store) is per-rig WORK domain — MUST stay plain in every sync/reconcile/release/buildDesired call; only the session `store`/SessionStore arg is routed.
4) MIRROR-OF-RUNTIME / TEST RIPPLE: every cascade helper is ALSO called by the daemon (city_runtime.go) and 10+ test files. The daemon already routes its session store independently (CityRuntime.sessionsBeadStore→resolveSessionStore), so routing at the cmd_start call sites needs NO helper signature change and no daemon/test edits. Tests pass nil cfg → identity, unaffected.
5) buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads is the one site that canNOT be surgically routed by arg-swap (session+work read the same store); attempting it here would either mis-route the work-demand arm or require a function split touching the daemon — a correctness risk if done piecemeal.
6) releaseOrphaned...'s embedded session read (liveOpenSessionAssignmentExists) stays on the plain work store; harmless today (identity) but is a known residual to note for E2.
e11.recommendation: Do the SURGICAL session-arm routing now: introduce a single `sessStore := cliSessionStore(oneShotStore, cfg, cityPath)` local computed right after oneShotStore is finalized (~:892), then route exactly 6 session-class sites — the two loadSessionBeadSnapshot reads (:896,:954), the three syncSessionBeadsWithSnapshotAndRigStores wraps (:907,:921,:962) as beads.SessionStore{Store: sessStore}, and the one reconcileSessionBeadsAtPathWithNamedDemand store arg (:942). Keep rigStores, releaseOrphaned…, filterAssignedWorkBeads*, configuredSessionNames* on plain. Explicitly DEFER the three buildDesiredStateWithSessionBeads sites (:902,:918,:959) — they need the shared build_desired_state.go two-store split done jointly with the daemon, not a cmd_start-local edit. Update the in-code comment at :876-879 and the frontdoor_di_guard_test.go note (:272-274) to reflect that the reconcile cascade's session arm is now routed while buildDesiredState remains the tracked follow-up. No helper signatures change; no other files/tests need edits.
